description:
You are an edge-computing engineer who has just pushed new firmware
  to a fleet of IoT sensors.  
  A deployment log already exists at  
  /home/user/device_deploy.log .

  Your goal: create a **single** summary file that tells you how
  many error events occurred during the rollout.

  Required work
  1. Scan /home/user/device_deploy.log for every line that contains
     the exact, case-sensitive tag “[ERROR]”.
  2. Count those lines.
  3. Write the **count only** (just the integer, no extra
     characters or whitespace other than the trailing newline) to the
     file  
     /home/user/error_count.log .  
     Example of correct file content if five errors were found:
       5⏎            ← the file ends with a single newline

  Output format rules (strictly enforced by automated tests)
  • The file /home/user/error_count.log must exist after you finish.  
  • It must contain exactly one line.  
  • That line must be a single non-negative integer representing the
    number of “[ERROR]” occurrences, immediately followed by a newline
    character (ASCII 0x0A).  
  • No leading or trailing spaces, tabs, or additional lines are
    allowed.

  Hints
  • You can solve this with one well-constructed shell command that
    combines a search utility with a line-counting option and redirects
    the result to the required file.
  • Do not modify /home/user/device_deploy.log itself.

  When you are done, the file /home/user/error_count.log will be used
  by automated grading scripts to verify that you produced the correct
  count in the exact format described above.
truth:
Pre-existing file:
  • Path: /home/user/device_deploy.log
  • Permissions: 644 (-rw-r--r--)
  • Contents (7 lines):
    1. 2023-10-01 10:00:00 [INFO]  Deployment started
    2. 2023-10-01 10:01:00 [ERROR] Connection timeout
    3. 2023-10-01 10:02:00 [INFO]  Retrying deployment
    4. 2023-10-01 10:03:00 [ERROR] Authentication failed
    5. 2023-10-01 10:04:00 [INFO]  Deployment aborted
    6. 2023-10-01 10:05:00 [ERROR] Rollback failed
    7. 2023-10-01 10:06:00 [INFO]  Support notified

  Verification data:
  • Correct error count: 3
  • After successful completion, /home/user/error_count.log must exist
    with mode 644 (-rw-r--r--) and contain exactly:
      3\n             (character “3” 0x33, newline 0x0A)
